topic: bangladesh budget proposals and financial news
headline_marked: [[Finance Minister to present 9.38 trillion BDT budget]] for FY2026-27 today
headline: Finance Minister to present 9.38 trillion BDT budget for FY2026-27 today
summary: This marks the first budget of the BNP government under Tarique Rahman's leadership. It faces a record deficit of BDT 2.43 trillion, despite prioritizing economic recovery and job creation.
details: Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury will present the national budget for the fiscal year 2026-27 today, Thursday, June 11, in the Jatiya Sangsad. The government plans a budget of BDT 9.38 trillion, marking the first budget of the BNP government under Tarique Rahman's leadership.
A significant aspect of this budget is the absence of any opportunity to whiten black money, as confirmed by the Chief Whip. This decision reflects a commitment to financial transparency.

The increased expenditure is driven by the need to tackle global risks, higher spending on subsidies and social security programs, pressure from interest payments, and the implementation of recommendations for a new pay structure for government employees.

A major challenge for the upcoming budget is a record deficit of BDT 2.43 trillion. Despite this, the government prioritizes economic recovery, boosting investment, and creating employment opportunities.
The budget sets a target to bring inflation down to 7.5% in the next fiscal year, with GDP growth projected at 6.5%. A substantial 67% of the funds are allocated to operating expenses.

Funds will also be directed towards the Annual Development Program (ADP), loans and advances, and food accounts. The National Board of Revenue (NBR) is expected to be the primary source of funding for budget implementation, followed by domestic and foreign loans, and other sources.
caption:
Bangladesh's Finance Minister is set to present a BDT 9.38 trillion national budget today, facing a record BDT 2.43 trillion deficit.

Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury will present the national budget for the fiscal year 2026-27 today, Thursday, June 11, in the Jatiya Sangsad. The government plans a budget of BDT 9.38 trillion, marking the first budget of the BNP government under Tarique Rahman's leadership.

A significant aspect of this budget is the absence of any opportunity to whiten black money, as confirmed by the Chief Whip. This decision reflects a commitment to financial transparency.

The increased expenditure is driven by the need to tackle global risks, higher spending on subsidies and social security programs, pressure from interest payments, and the implementation of recommendations for a new pay structure for government employees.

A major challenge for the upcoming budget is a record deficit of BDT 2.43 trillion. Despite this, the government prioritizes economic recovery, boosting investment, and creating employment opportunities.

The budget sets a target to bring inflation down to 7.5% in the next fiscal year, with GDP growth projected at 6.5%. A substantial 67% of the funds are allocated to operating expenses.

Funds will also be directed towards the Annual Development Program (ADP), loans and advances, and food accounts. The National Board of Revenue (NBR) is expected to be the primary source of funding for budget implementation, followed by domestic and foreign loans, and other sources.

Source: Jamuna TV, Somoy News
